content: We can frame Self-Refine's iterative improvement framework as a structural backbone that solves a key unaddressed gap in existing LLM self-reflection mechanisms: it enables multiple-perspective reflection (like the inconsistent perspectives used in Self-Contrast or Mirror) without requiring additional fine-tuning or auxiliary supervised data. A multi-perspective Self-Refine would first generate outputs from different inconsistent reasoning perspectives, then iteratively critique and refine each against the others using the LLM's own self-feedback capability.
